REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANT THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. MOVING PARTY FAILED TO SHIFT ITS BURDEN RE: PROPER TERMINATION UPON LANDLORD'S DEFAULT AFTER EFFECTIVE NOTICE. WHILE PLAINTIFF PROVIDES EVIDENCE THAT A NOTICE WAS PROVIDED, THERE IS NO EVIDENCE THAT AN EFFECTIVE NOTICE UNDER THE LEASE WAS PROVIDED. PLAINTIFF'S PURPORTED ADMISSIONS DOES NOT ADMIT "EFFECTIVE" NOTICE. FURTHERMORE, THERE ARE TRIABLE ISSUES OF FACT REGARDING PLAINTIFF'S COMPLIANCE WITH ITS OBLIGATIONS UNDER THE LEASE RE: THE EXTENT AND QUALITY OF THE ONGOING AND/OR PERIODIC REPAIRS/MAINTENANCE. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES; CLERK: J. MURPHY; REPORTED: DECEMBER MOORE, 415.328.6644; DMORRE@BAYAREATRIALREPORTERS.COM. (501)

Argued. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is DENIED. Moving party failed to shift its burden re: proper termination upon landlord's default after effective notice. While Plaintiff provides evidence that a notice was provided, there is no evidence that an effective notice under the lease was provided. Plaintiff's purported admissions does not admit "effective" notice. Furthermore, there are triable issues of fact regarding Plaintiff's compliance with its obligations under the lease re: the extent and quality of the ongoing and/or periodic repairs/maintenance. Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: J. Murphy; Not Reported: Reported: December Moore, 415.328.6644; dmorre@bayareatrialreporters.com. Without an expert report or deposition testimony, the Court cannot determine whether Defendant's expert Mr. Flemming's anticipated testimony will address only "subject[s] to be covered by" Plaintiff's expert Mr. Colton, which would be permitted by CCP 2034.280, or would address subjects not covered by Mr. Colton, which would not. Defendant must make Mr. Flemming available for deposition before Mr. Colton's trial testimony so that the trial judge may consider, based on a properly developed record, any renewed motion to preclude Mr. Flemming from testifying, or to limit his testimony. The requests for sanctions by both parties are DENIED. Plaintiff's request is denied because the motion is denied (albeit without prejudice). Defendant's request is denied because Plaintiff's request to make Mr. Flemming available for deposition is granted.
The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and submit by email to sborrowman@gmail.com. Judge Pro Tem Scott Borrowman; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/JPT)
